I absolutely loved this museum. It was very informative, had a great museography and a very complete and interesting exhibition. the staff was kind and enthusiastic, and the graphic and visual materials were stunning. everything is clean and the entrance is free. the shop was also cool. my only observation is that the name should honestly be changed. native americans are not indians.

This is a really nice museum and it's one that I never knew was near Battery Park. Admission is free as it's a Smithsonian museum and they have a large variety of artifacts inside that span centuries. You can spend several hours here and it's really a nice museum. The building itself is also gorgeous and I would definitely recommend visiting here!

When we visited Liberty Island, we learned a little about the Native Americans who used to go to the island for fishing. Wanting to learn more, we visited this museum. It’s definitely worth visiting, and provides an insight into the history of the area. It was reassuring to see evidence of the museum working with indigenous people - I noticed a couple of exhibits had been removed at the request of native communities. I only wish it was bigger and provided more information on the experiences of native Americans from colonisation to present day.
